text: N-Acetyl-L-Tryptophan (L-AcW), chemical formula C13H14N2O3, molecular weight 246.26, is a pure organic molecular crystal, an N-terminally acetylated aromatic amino acid. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, chiral orthorhombic space group P212121 (No. 19), with unit cell a = 7.470 Å, b = 25.912 Å, c = 6.247 Å, Z = 4, volume 1209.19 Å³, determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 6.0% (CCDC 1100754, CSD refcode ACLTRY). Within the crystal each L-AcW molecule is connected to two adjacent molecules by two hydrogen bonds, and two tail-to-tail molecules stack in layers along the crystallographic c direction. In a 2023 supramolecular-engineering study, density functional theory (DFT) predicted that L-AcW assemblies exhibit the highest piezoelectric response among 15 acetylated amino acid crystals, with a maximum shear piezoelectric strain coefficient d25 of 47.3 pm/V and a piezoelectric voltage constant g25 of 1719 mV m/N, values comparable to inorganic bismuth triborate crystals and exceeding lead zirconium titanate ceramics. An L-AcW crystal-based piezoelectric nanogenerator produced a stable open-circuit voltage of over 1.4 V (up to 1.46 V at 52 N) under mechanical pressure and, for the first time in an amino-acid-based device, powered the illumination of an LED, highlighting L-AcW as a promising building block for piezoelectric energy harvesting and sensing.
property_ref_title: Manipulating the Piezoelectric Response of Amino Acid-Based Assemblies by Supramolecular Engineering
property_ref_journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
